overview.short: Kamalgad (literally lotus fort), also called Bhelanja or Kattalgad (literally death fort) is a square hill fort in Wai Taluka in Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: Kamalgad (literally lotus fort), also called Bhelanja or Kattalgad (literally death fort) is a square hill fort in Wai Taluka in Maharashtra, India.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kondhawali
topPlaces.description: Kondhawali, also written Kondhavale or Kondhavali, is a village in the Satara District of Maharashtra state, India.

topPlaces.name: Dhom Dam
topPlaces.description: Dhom Dam is an earthfill and gravity dam on Krishna River near Wai in state of Maharashtra in India.

topPlaces.name: Bhilar
topPlaces.description: Bhilar is a village in Satara district in the Indian state of Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Panchgani
topPlaces.description: Panchgani, called Paachgani, is a hill station and municipal council in Satara district in Maharashtra, India. Panchgani attracts tourists throughout the year.

topPlaces.name: Venna Lake
topPlaces.description: Venna Lake is one of the tourist attractions of Mahabaleshwar in Maharashtra state in India. The lake was constructed in 1842 by Shahaji, the last raja (king) of the Satara princely state.

topPlaces.name: Raireshwar
topPlaces.description: Raireshwar Fort is in Bhor taluka near Pune, India, 82 km (51 mi) away. It is between various hills and forts, such as Kenjalgad.

items.note: Kondhawali, also written Kondhavale or Kondhavali, is a village in the Satara District of Maharashtra state, India.
Dhom Dam is an earthfill and gravity dam on Krishna River near Wai in state of Maharashtra in India.
Bhilar is a village in Satara district in the Indian state of Maharashtra.
Panchgani, called Paachgani, is a hill station and municipal council in Satara district in Maharashtra, India. Panchgani attracts tourists throughout the year.
Venna Lake is one of the tourist attractions of Mahabaleshwar in Maharashtra state in India. The lake was constructed in 1842 by Shahaji, the last raja (king) of the Satara princely state.
Raireshwar Fort is in Bhor taluka near Pune, India, 82 km (51 mi) away. It is between various hills and forts, such as Kenjalgad.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kamalgad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kamalgad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Pune Airport (71 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (64 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kamalgad?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kamalgad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kamalgad?
faq.a: Stays in Kamalgad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kamalgad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kondhawali, Dhom Dam, Bhilar, Panchgani, Venna Lake.
